q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is os.system(cmd) on line 43, which is shell execution.

mock_guidance.target: Mock os.system to prevent actual shell execution during testing.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the exact string argument passed to os.system, storing it for assertion so the injected command payload can be verified.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return 0 (success) without executing the command, allowing the function under test to complete normally.
notes: Since os.system is the direct sink, patching it at the os module level (e.g., unittest.mock.patch('os.system')) is sufficient.
Assert that the captured argument contains the malicious payload derived from user_input to confirm the vulnerability.
